original_hypothesis.title: Anyone can spend from any address in test token via transferFrom allowance bypass; if used as staking token it enables direct theft
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: GTC.sol’s transferFrom() explicitly removes the allowance check (“Removed allowance check for easy testing”). If this token is mistakenly used as the live staking token, any attacker can transfer tokens from any holder to themselves and then stake/withdraw normally, or drain user wallets directly.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This is the most catastrophic failure mode if test artifacts are deployed by mistake. The staking contract relies on transferFrom, so the blast radius includes both direct wallet theft and fraudulent staking/reputation manipulation.
deployment_assumptions: GTC.sol (test mock) is deployed/used as the token in a production or public environment
Victims hold balances of that token
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: GTC.sol header states test-only; however no deployment script for IdentityStaking is present, and deploy.ts is unrelated, so artifact selection controls should be verified.

proof_of_signal.pattern: transferFrom(): // Removed allowance check for easy testing

preconditions: Mock GTC token is deployed on a public chain and used by real users

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: ERC20 allowance model is intentionally broken

operational_questions: Are there safeguards preventing the mock token from being deployed/used outside tests?
Is IdentityStaking configured to use the canonical GTC token on each target chain?
validation_requirements: Verify token address used in initialize() on deployed instances
Bytecode-compare token contract at that address to canonical GTC implementation

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Call transferFrom(victim, attacker, amount) directly on the token
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: Optionally stake stolen tokens to fabricate reputation signals or withdraw later

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Total loss of user funds in the token; reputational system compromised
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: Very high; requires token migration and incident response
potential_operational_impact.affected_operations: All token holders and any staking/reputation integration

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: transferFrom calls where msg.sender is not approved; unusual transfers from many unrelated EOAs

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
